danger_signals.visual_form: Assorted enemy sprites positioned as obstacles in level geometry
danger_signals.meaning: Hazards that must be avoided or neutralized; represent threats to Woody's progression

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual gaps, drops, and environmental hazards between player and goal
danger_signals.meaning: Spatial danger requiring platforming skill to traverse safely

danger_signals.visual_form: Draining battery meter in overhead-view racing segments
danger_signals.meaning: Resource scarcity creating time pressure; failure state if batteries depleted

danger_signals.visual_form: Countdown timer visible during first-person maze navigation
danger_signals.meaning: Temporal pressure; communicates urgency of objective completion

danger_signals.visual_form: Unique boss variant distinct from standard enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Narrative manifestation of Woody's anxiety; climactic danger requiring whip-based combat

safety_signals.visual_form: Woody's equipped weapon asset; interaction mechanic with enemies and environmental hooks
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency against threats; enables temporary enemy neutralization and environmental traversal via hook-swinging

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible items found in overhead racing levels
safety_signals.meaning: Resource replenishment; extends player's resource window and reduces failure risk

safety_signals.visual_form: NPC character who dislikes confrontation; narratively helps Woody escape
safety_signals.meaning: Social safety; faction support enabling escape from threat (angered toys)

safety_signals.visual_form: Sequential stage layout following film plot linearly
safety_signals.meaning: Narrative clarity and guided progression reduce disorientation and systemic confusion

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Toys appear as enemies when angered at Woody after Buzz is knocked out window
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Andy's room setting; toy-scale interior environments; furniture-based level geometry

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Space suit design; sci-fi toy aesthetic distinct from cowboy Woody
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Nightmare Buzz—a boss-level manifestation of Woody's anxious projection, visually distinguished from standard encounters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Space-themed visual elements associated with Buzz's character design; claw machine area (Pizza Planet) where Buzz mistakes rocket for escape vehicle

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Alien toy design; claw machine residents
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Claw machine interior; Pizza Planet location; first-person maze environment communicating alien territory
Gas station setting; moving van; Pizza Planet location; human-scale environments contrasting toy perspective

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Andy's room in organized state; toys in neutral positions; controlled environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Green army men conducting reconnaissance via baby monitor; toys maintaining cover positions; orderly patrol behavior

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Buzz's impressive visual design and space suit contrasting with Woody's simpler cowboy aesthetic; environmental focus on Buzz in room
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Toys gravitating toward Buzz; competitive race challenge between Buzz and Woody; other toys impressed with newcomer

world_state_to_visual.world_state: After Buzz knocked out window (toys angered, Woody isolated)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Toys positioned as blocking obstacles/enemies; environmental hazards increased; Woody separated from allies visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Toys pursue Woody as enemies; Rex aids Woody's escape; Woody forced to navigate hostile toy environment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shift to gas station, Pizza Planet, moving van, and claw machine environments; scale and aesthetic change to reflect human world intrusion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Woody and Buzz must disguise themselves; navigation of human spaces requires stealth; collaborative problem-solving

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Inside claw machine (alien containment, maze navigation)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: First-person perspective shift; maze geometry; alien toy residents; time pressure indicator
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Squeaky toy aliens task Woody with rescue objective; navigation requires spatial puzzle-solving; resource management (time limit)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Nightmare Buzz boss encounter—distinct visual variant of Buzz designed as climactic threat
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss battle requiring whip-based combat; permanent defeat possible (only enemy defeated this way); represents internal psychological state made gameplay-real

environmental_storytelling.example: 18-level (Genesis) stage sequence follows Toy Story film plot linearly: birthday party → Buzz arrival → conflict → escape from house → Pizza Planet infiltration → claw machine maze → resolution. Environmental transition communicates plot advancement.

environmental_storytelling.technique: Scale and perspective shifts for world-state clarity
environmental_storytelling.example: Overhead view for R.C. racing stages (toy-scale vehicle navigation); first-person maze for claw machine interior (immersive alien environment); side-scrolling platformer for standard toy-scale traversal. Perspective communicates context and threat type.
After Buzz is knocked out window, toys become enemies blocking Woody's path. Visual transformation of allies into obstacles communicates social consequence and escalated stakes.
Andy's room levels (familiar toy space) contrast with gas station, Pizza Planet, and claw machine interiors (alien/human territory). Visual shift communicates displacement and danger increase.
Battery depletion meter in R.C. racing stages; time limit in claw machine maze. Visual UI elements communicate systemic pressure and world-state (resource scarcity = heightened danger).
Nightmare Buzz is visually distinct boss variant appearing mid-game, representing Woody's internal anxiety made tangible. Design communicates emotional world-state through gameplay entity.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Equipped with pullstring whip; can swing on hooks; can temporarily immobilize enemies; proactive level navigation
Encounters Nightmare Buzz boss; must utilize whip defensively; confronted by angered toys forcing escape
Wins initial race against Woody; flies with eyes closed; impresses other toys; visually prominent in early stages
Serves as climactic boss enemy; can be permanently defeated via whip (unique to this enemy); manifests Woody's jealousy/anxiety
Gravitate toward Buzz; challenge Woody; assist in games/races
Transform into enemies; block Woody's path; chase behavior
Dislikes confrontation but assists Woody's escape; communicates moral support despite danger
Requires battery resource management; overhead perspective; acceleration/braking/steering mechanics; communicates resource scarcity threat
Greet Woody inside claw machine; task him with rescue objective; imply social structure within alien toy group
Conduct baby-monitor patrol during birthday party setup; early-game communication of organized toy society structure

notes: Toy Story Genesis communicates world-state primarily through spatial level design, perspective shifts, and enemy/ally transformations rather than explicit UI overlays. The game's manifestation layer is driven by: (1) Narrative-paced progression (level sequence mirrors film plot), (2) Mechanical shifts (platformer → overhead racing → first-person maze) that communicate different contexts, (3) Enemy roster transformation (toys shift from allies to obstacles), and (4) Boss design as psychological manifestation (Nightmare Buzz represents internal anxiety). Danger is communicated through perilous terrain, enemy obstacles, and resource meters (battery/time). Safety is conveyed through tool availability (whip), ally assistance (Rex), and procedural progression. Faction identity relies on toy aesthetic and spatial association rather than color/sigil differentiation in documented sources. The game lacks explicit faction UI but uses environmental storytelling (Andy's room vs. external human spaces) to communicate threat context.
